mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Azure provider with v7.2.1 and ADAL stop working - Access token validation failure. Invalid audience
mission.description: Azure provider with v7.2.1 and ADAL stop working - Access token validation failure. Invalid audience. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
After the update from `v7.2.0` to `v7.2.1` the integration with the AAD stops working.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Azure provider with v7.2.1 and ADAL stop working - Access…
steps.description:
any update on the fix?
```yaml
[2022/01/06 13:32:29] [internal_util.go:74] token validation request failed: status 401 - {"error":{"code":"InvalidAuthenticationToken","message":"Access token validation failure. Invalid audience.","innerError":{"date":"2022-01-06T13:32:29","request-id":"[REDACTED]","client-request-id":"[REDACTED]"}}}
[2022/01/06 13:32:29] [internal_util.go:69] 401 GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me {"error":{"code":"InvalidAuthenticationToken","message":"Access token validation failure. Invalid audience.","innerError":{"date":"2022-01-06T13:32:29","request-id":"[REDACTED]","client-request-id":"[REDACTED
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Azure provider with v7.2.1 and ADAL stop working - Access…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy/issues/1505

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
